item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree, it's narratively more interesting to figure it out before it's explained. That does break format somewhat, but I gave it +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering if the first words in both "the following text" and "this money was" should maybe be capitalised? Not sure if they're proper errors, or just my personal preference, though.

I really like this idea, both the mind-affecting elements, and the visual aspects of the lollipops and the subject's tongue during Barnum Event.
I can also see the potential for a fun tale around Lolly from Dread & Circuses getting her hands on one of these and causing chaos for a few hours! Perhaps they were disquietly discontinued for this very reason?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually generally don't like skips based on "hey, what if…" pun exposition, but this works. It explores it in a convincing way that fits the GoI and has a good pacing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The primary effect is such a nice idea. It's a Wonka-esque fantasy candy that leaves me genuinely miffed that science has yet to bring such miracles to life. And the pun that ties the primary and secondary effects together is simply brilliant. The Barnum incident is the kind of justifiable lolfoundation we need more of. Oh, and it's a nice change of pace to see a Circus article with nothing overtly spoopy going on. Hearty +1

Hello guys, gals and non-binary pals!

I am a filthy liberal and I strongly approve of this message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best kind of SCP imho is one that takes something that you don't think about very much and thinks about it waaaaaaaaaaaaay too much. Whether that be an abstract concept or a strange idiom anything that gives me that "ooooh that's interesting" moment gets my upvote 100%.

So uh, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any article that gets a genuine out-loud emotional reaction out of me is deserving of an upvote. And this one had me giggling like a looney, especially when the pun dawned on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded, though I didn't realize this until you pointed it out. I guess whenever I see a [WHATEVER] event, I've just been conditioned to expect details behind a Level X Clearance Required collapsible. +1 anyway, that dialogue with the D Class was funny as hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this SCP has quite a simple effect and goes in quite an obvious direction with it. The pun isn't enough to save it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how D-97605 figures out what's up and just runs with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked how we got to see a Barnum Event before we're told what it is. Realizing what the anomalous effect was from reading the narrative is a special thrill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I clicked on this hoping it was a pun, and was really happy that it was something other than the horror-leech-based pun that I was thinking of. (I'm so done with parasitic-leech-based skips you have no idea)

As others have said, the organization of the addenda is a little weird and definitely nonstandard in such a way that I don't think the in-universe file would be organized that way, but I think I know what you were going for and honestly? I enjoyed the brief "what?" moment in-between assuming the doctor was going along with the D's joke and realizing that he wasn't. That kind of storytelling definitely doesn't work for everyone, I think, but I liked it.

It's simple and a little cliche but everything can't be a damn Shyamalan movie so as far as I'm concerned, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? I read that whole interview with a big stupid grin on my face, and that's enough for me to upvote. Well done :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry. Didn’t enjoy it due to the unrealistic organisation of the addenda and the predictable simplicity of the core anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Random - Yes, I realize that in-universe it would make more sense for the description of the Barnum Event to be in the description, and it was a narrative choice to place it afterwards. My beta readers generally liked the experience of figuring out what was going on as they were reading it, and describing the Barnum Event before the test would rob them of that.

For the sake of the narrative, I'm willing to believe that the researcher who updated this had written everything after the experiment separately and just copy pasted it into the main document to minimize his workload. I'm sure he'll get called out on his sloppiness eventually.

Speaking of beta readers, they were Bear-run Eads fieldstone Lazar Lyusternik
mayoculpa stormbreath Superblobby and taylor_itkin. Thanks for your help!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, that pun-chline. Upvoted - whether despite, or because of, the pun I leave to the imagination of the reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The organization of the addendums is sloppy: although you may have done it for pacing slash plot progression, I shouldn't have to read past the first incident of a Barnum Event to know what a Barnum Event actually is.
